public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as ward notary.
appearance: Bright-plumed road gear, polished fittings, and visible attention to presentation mark the role before the voice does.
manner: Projects readiness without theatricality, as if discipline matters more than spectacle. In Shelk, that reads as someone polished, socially alert, and attentive to harmony, status, and cultural presentation, and the habits of a ward notary still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of ward notary in and around Ironspool Ward District, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Ironspool Ward District workable through the obligations of ward notary while managing the immediate pressure of desperation contracts in Shelk.
hidden_pressure: Must prepare for violence without letting the surrounding institution look openly unstable. Around Ironspool Ward District, that usually means carrying desperation contracts, petty crime, omens and superstition without letting the role of ward notary visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Thalen Petalglass needs help hardening a position, drilling unreliable auxiliaries, or reading an approaching threat around Ironspool Ward District.
Thalen Petalglass can pull the party into a dispute over readiness, chain of command, or blamed failure near Ironspool Ward District.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained. Appearance extended under V28_8 from role/locality/public-signal derivation rather than decorative fabrication.
public_signal: Weapon discipline, drilled stillness, and practiced readiness mark Thalen Petalglass as someone tied to organized force rather than casual violence. In Ironspool Ward District, this lands inside a public field already shaped by local warders, repair guilds.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Ironspool Ward District: drill yards, fortifications, command posts, or exposed routes
